title: 10 best herbal essential oils for jaw clenching
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for jaw clenching are plant-based oils that help alleviate the symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, a condition characterized by involuntary jaw clenching and grinding. They work by promoting relaxation, reducing muscle tension, and easing pain. The best essential oils for jaw clenching include Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Boswellia serrata, Cinnamomum verum, Passiflora incarnata, Santalum album, and Cymbopogon citratus. If left untreated, jaw clenching can lead to headaches, tooth damage, and sleep disturbances, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the face and jaw. The oil's gentle, peaceful aroma can also have a profound impact on reducing stress and anxiety, common triggers for teeth grinding and clenching. As the body releases tension, the jaw muscles release their grip, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable bite. Regular use of English lavender essential oils can lead to improved oral health and reduced discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Lavender oil's sedative properties can help to calm the nervous system, reducing tension and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic properties of lavender oil can help to alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw, neck, and face associated with teeth grinding and clenching.
Antispasmodic properties: Lavender oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles in the face, jaw, and neck, reducing muscle spasms and cramping that may contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are known for their calming properties, which can help alleviate jaw tension and clenching by promoting relaxation.
Buds: The buds of the English lavender plant contain concentrated oils that are effective in reducing stress and anxiety, common causes of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves and flowers at dawn for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Steam distill 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia with 1000 milliliters of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the condenser trap and store it in amber glass bottles at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 14 days before use or bottling to enhance its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with jaw clenching because it contains a calming agent that relaxes the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw. The herb has a long history of use as a natural remedy for stress-related tension, which often contributes to jaw clenching. When used topically or inhaled, lemon balm essential oil can ease muscle spasms and promote a sense of calm, helping to reduce habitual jaw clenching and promote overall facial relaxation.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil's sedative properties can help relax tense muscles and calm nervous energy, which often contributes to jaw clenching.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of lemon balm essential oil can help reduce muscle spasms and cramps in the face, neck, and jaw that are associated with jaw clenching.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw and surrounding areas, which can exacerbate jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to calm and soothe the nervous system, reducing tension and stress that can lead to jaw clenching.
Leaves: Leaves contain a high concentration of citral, a natural relaxant that helps to ease muscle tension and promote relaxation, alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn on a dry morning and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Combine the rinsed melissa officinalis leaves with 50 grams of pure vodka in a clean glass jar and store it for 21 days.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids then add 100 milliliters of carrier oil.
Steep the liquid mixture in a warm dark place at room temperature for 24 hours to allow infusion.
Filter the infused oil again through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its natural ability to ease muscle tension and reduce inflammation. The oil's antioxidant properties help to relax the jaw muscles, calming the tension that can lead to clenching. Additionally, sage oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to soothe the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, sage essential oils provide a natural remedy for alleviating jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal sage essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with jaw clenching and tension headaches, allowing for a more relaxed jaw and facial muscles.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of herbal sage essential oil can help relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to jaw clenching.

Stress-relieving: Herbal sage essential oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger jaw clenching and other oral habits like teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help soothe jaw tension.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes are rich in bioactive compounds that can help reduce muscle spasms and relax the jaw muscles, providing relief from clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when dewy for optimal oil yield and quality.
Dry harvested leaves using air circulation method at 60°c temperature for 3-4 hours.
Steam distill dried leaves in a stainless steel still with distilled water ratio of 10:1 for 4-6 hours.
Filter extracted oil through activated charcoal and then fractionate using fractional distillation column for 2 hours.
Bottle final essential oil in dark glass bottle with tight lid, store at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The oil's potent compounds, such as calendulic acid and triterpenoids, work together to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. As a result, massaging pot marigold essential oils into the affected area can help alleviate jaw clenching, promoting relaxation and relief from pain and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Marigold oil helps relax tense muscles, including those in the jaw, reducing spasms and alleviating symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain caused by jaw clenching, making it an effective natural remedy for treating facial and oral discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Marigold oil's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to muscle tension and joint pain, promoting overall relaxation and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw, making them effective for relieving jaw clenching.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-spasmodic properties, which can help relax tense muscles in the face and relieve tension-related jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 100g of dried calendula flowers with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place allowing the calendula to infuse into the oil.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Store the resulting essential oil blend in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its natural soothing properties that can calm the mind and body. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds present in chamomile oil can relax the muscles, including those in the face, neck, and jaw. When applied topically to the affected area, chamomile oil can reduce tension and ease muscle cramps, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable jaw position. This natural remedy can be particularly effective in addressing teeth grinding, facial pain, and TMJ disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant: Chamomile essential oil's muscle-relaxing properties help to calm tensed muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing tension and spasms that can lead to jaw clenching.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching and teeth grinding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in soothing compounds that can help calm tension and relax the muscles, making them ideal for relieving jaw clenching.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a steam distillation apparatus and collect the resulting essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight and heat for 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava essential oils helps with jaw clenching because it contains compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn relaxes the muscles of the face and jaw. The sedative properties of kava oil help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw clenching. Additionally, kava's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate tension headaches and facial pain caused by chronic teeth grinding or clenching, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Kava's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle relaxant effects can help ease tension in the jaw muscles, reducing the likelihood of clenching and grinding.
Pain-relieving properties: Kava's analgesic (pain-reducing) properties can help alleviate jaw pain and discomfort associated with bruxism, which is often accompanied by jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of sesquiterpenes, which help to relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain high levels of kavalactones, which have been shown to effectively reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh kava root samples and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the kava roots in a warm, dark place for at least three days.
Grind the dried kava roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 20 grams of the ground kava powder with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and stir well.
Store the mixture in an amber glass bottle and allow it to steep for four weeks before straining and decanting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with jaw clenching because its calming properties help to relax the muscles in the face, neck, and shoulders, which are often linked to jaw tension. The citrus scent of lemongrass oil also has a natural analgesic effect, reducing discomfort and pain caused by clenched teeth and jaws. By applying lemongrass essential oil to the temples or neck, individuals can experience relief from jaw clenching, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation throughout the body.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, including those in the jaw that can lead to clenching.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint, which can contribute to clenching and discomfort.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: Helps to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may trigger jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain compounds that help relax the muscles and reduce tension, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for jaw clenching relief.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves measuring 10-12 inches long.
Dry the harvested lemongrass in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 85°f for 6-8 hours.
Crush 100 grams of dried lemongrass into small pieces to release essential oils and enzymes.
Steam-distill the crushed lemongrass with 2 liters of water in a stainless steel still for 30-40 minutes, collecting the resulting oil in a glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities, resulting in 10-15 milliliters of pure lemongrass essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its natural relaxing properties that calm the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw. The oil's warm, sweet aroma is said to ease tension and reduce stress, common triggers for clenching and grinding teeth. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce discomfort and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. By applying the oil to the temples, forehead, or jawline, individuals can experience relief from jaw tension and promote a more relaxed, healthy smile.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Fennel essential oil helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can ease tension in the jaw and alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) and bruxism.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw clenching, making it easier to manage conditions like TMJ.
Carminative property: Fennel essential oil's ability to dispel gas and relieve flatulence can also help alleviate digestive issues, which are often linked to jaw tension and clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of volatile compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce tension, making them effective in calming jaw clenching.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the early morning for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop 20 grams of the harvested material into small pieces for efficient distillation.
Combine the chopped plant material with 100 milliliters of water in a distillation apparatus for a 3-hour steaming process.
Collect the resulting essential oil by condensing and separating it from the steam using a cooling system within an hour.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for up to 6 months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as cineole and borneol, have a soothing effect on the muscles and nerves in the face and jaw, helping to ease tension and reduce stress that often leads to jaw clenching. Additionally, marjoram's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJDs), allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable bite.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory: Marjoram essential oil has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve pain and reduce swelling associated with jaw clenching, making it an effective natural remedy for TMJ disorders.
Muscle relaxant: The relaxing effects of marjoram essential oil can help soothe tense muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, which is often a contributing factor to jaw clenching and grinding.
Sedative and calming: Marjoram essential oil has sedative properties that can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which can help relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Leaves: Abundant in cineole, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties, effective in soothing jaw pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of essential oil production.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set at 65°f for 6-8 hours or by air drying for several days.
Crush dried origanum majorana leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their oils.
Steam distill crushed leaves with 10% water ratio for 2-3 hours using a steam distillation apparatus to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a coffee filter or cheesecloth within 30 minutes of production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its unique properties that promote relaxation and ease muscle tension. The citrusy scent of lime oil has a calming effect on the mind, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to jaw clenching. Additionally, the menthol content in lime oil helps to cool and soothe the muscles, relieving discomfort and stiffness associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. As the tension melts away, jaw clenching subsides, allowing for a more relaxed and comfortable jaw movement.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal lime oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, can help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints, providing relief from tension and pain caused by clenching.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The calming and soothing effects of herbal lime oil can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for jaw clenching and teeth grinding (bruxism).

Muscle relaxant properties: Herbal lime oil's muscle-relaxing compounds, such as citral and geranial, can help calm and relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing tension and spasms that can lead to clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lime roots contain calming compounds that help relax muscles, making them effective in reducing jaw tension and clenching.
Barks: The bark of the lime tree contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and reduce pain caused by jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh tilia x europaea leaves in the morning for maximum oil content preservation.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a clevenger apparatus with a water-to-material ratio of 4:1 and collect the essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or contaminants within 30 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for a minimum of 24 hours before use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw clenching with herbal essential oils is to blend lavender, peppermint, and bergamot oils in a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil. Massage the blend onto your temples, neck, and jawline to relax tense muscles. Inhale deeply, focusing on slow, deliberate breaths to calm the mind and body. Regular use can help alleviate symptoms of bruxism and promote overall relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal essential oils are various stress-related conditions, such as anxiety, insomnia, and migraines. The calming properties of herbal essential oils like lavender, chamomile, and bergamot help soothe tense muscles and promote relaxation. Additionally, essential oils like peppermint and eucalyptus can also alleviate symptoms of tension headaches, while clary sage oil can help reduce stress and anxiety-induced muscle contractions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can help reduce muscle tension and stress. The oil's sedative effects can calm the mind and body, leading to a decrease in jaw clenching. Additionally, its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching, providing relief and promoting overall well-being. This makes it a popular natural remedy for managing bruxism and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A monoterpene alcohol that helps reduce muscle tension and promotes relaxation, which in turn can alleviate jaw clenching caused by stress or anxiety.
Linalyl acetate: A monoterpenic ester that exhibits sedative and calming effects, helping to reduce muscle spasms and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate jaw clenching.
Limonene: A monoterpene that has been shown to have anxiolytic and muscle-relaxing properties, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the body and can help relax tense muscles.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, alleviating jaw clenching.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain a moderate amount of linalool and linalyl acetate, which can contribute to a calming and relaxing effect on the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to capture optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers using a distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its unique decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in eucalyptus oil relaxes tense facial muscles and calms the mind, reducing jaw clenching and grinding. Additionally, its natural anxiolytic properties help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, eucalyptus oil provides a natural solution for individuals suffering from jaw clenching, helping to restore a healthy and balanced bite.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with jaw clenching by acting as a natural analgesic, reducing pain and inflammation in the jaw muscles.
Eucalyptol: Contributes to relieving jaw clenching by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax tense muscles.
Bornyl acetate: Plays a role in alleviating jaw clenching by displaying analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby reducing discomfort and pain in the jaw area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are most commonly used to make essential oils for jaw clenching relief due to their high eucalyptol content, which has natural pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.

Barks: Eucalyptus globulus bark is also used to make essential oils for jaw clenching relief because it contains a similar composition of eucalyptol as the leaves, providing similar pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory benefits.
Stems: Some essential oils from Eucalyptus globulus stems are used for jaw clenching relief due to their mild eucalyptol content, although they may not be as potent as oils from leaves or barks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present for freshness.
Steam distill 100g of eucalyptus globulus leaves with 5l of distilled water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the condensed steam and separate it from the water using a condenser and separator for 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any remaining particulate matter for 30 minutes.
Store the eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 20°c for long-term preservation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax muscles. The oil, also known as tea tree oil, contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol that have a calming effect on the body. When inhaled, it can help reduce tension and promote relaxation, easing jaw clenching and related discomfort. Additionally, its decongestant properties can help alleviate sinus pressure, further contributing to a reduction in jaw tension and clenching.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with jaw clenching by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the jaw muscles.
Linalool: Contributes to reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to jaw clenching, and also has a soothing effect on the jaw muscles.
α-pinene: Exhibits anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the underlying causes of jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate jaw pain and tension.
Buds: They are often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Stems: They may be used to create essential oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe jaw pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with jaw clenching because it promotes relaxation and reduces muscle tension. The calming properties of valerian root oil can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common causes of jaw clenching. By soothing the nervous system, valerian oil can calm the mind and body, leading to a decrease in jaw tension. Additionally, its sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted by chronic jaw clenching, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Helps to reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles, alleviating jaw clenching and associated anxiety.
Valerenic acid: Exhibits anxiolytic properties, which can help to decrease anxiety-induced muscle tension and promote relaxation of the jaw muscles.
Valeranone: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, leading to a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and relax the jaw muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid, which is used to treat anxiety and insomnia, and also helps in relaxing the muscles, including the jaw.
Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis contain a higher concentration of valerenic acid, which helps in reducing muscle tension and spasms, including jaw clenching.

Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis contain a combination of valerenic acid and other volatile compounds, which helps in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, thus reducing jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in early spring or fall when the plant is dormant to ensure highest quality.
Clean and dry the roots in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to prevent moisture buildup.
Steam distill 100g of valeriana officinalis roots with 1 liter of distilled water for 2-3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol by decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The oil contains compounds such as linalool and citral, which have a soothing effect on the nervous system. When inhaled, Melissa oil can help reduce stress and anxiety, common causes of jaw clenching. Its muscle-relaxing properties also help alleviate tension in the jaw and facial muscles, promoting relaxation and reducing the frequency of jaw clenching. Regular use may lead to improved overall well-being and reduced symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has a relaxing effect on the muscles and nervous system, which can help alleviate jaw clenching and associated tension.
Geraniol: This terpene has a calming and sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the jaw muscles and surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high content of lemon balm oil, which can help relax muscles and reduce stress.

Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are also used to make essential oils, as they contain compounds that can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which may help reduce jaw clenching.

Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are occasionally used to make essential oils, although less commonly than the leaves and flowers, as they can provide a more subtle and balanced effect when used in aromatherapy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers when they are in full bloom and have reached their maximum fragrance potential.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a distillation apparatus with a 20 kg batch and 3 hours distillation time.
Collect the essential oil that has separated from the distillation apparatus and store it in a clean glass container.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities before bottling and labeling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce tension and pain in the jaw and facial muscles. The oil's ability to relax and calm the nervous system also plays a role in alleviating jaw clenching. Additionally, its analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties help to soothe and ease muscle spasms, promoting relaxation and reducing the frequency of jaw clenching episodes. This makes it a popular natural remedy for those experiencing TMJ disorders.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw clenching and related conditions.
Terpineol: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.

3-O-Acyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (3-O-AKBA): It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can contribute to reducing jaw clenching and related tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw clenching pain and tension.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, they contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce jaw clenching discomfort.
Barks: The bark of Boswellia serrata contains boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw clenching pain and tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of dried boswellia serrata resin from certified organic sources.
Steam distill the dried resin at 70-80 degrees celsius for 4-6 hours using a 10-liter distillation apparatus.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove any impurities.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper top and label it clearly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's warm, spicy aroma can help relax the jaw muscles and ease tension, reducing clenching and grinding. Its ability to calm the nervous system also contributes to its effectiveness in managing stress-related jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Cinnamomum verum essential oils can provide relief from jaw clenching and promote a sense of overall well-being. Regular use can lead to improved oral health and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
Linalool: Linalool has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde has a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help to reduce muscle tension in the jaw and surrounding areas, alleviating jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are used to make essential oils for jaw clenching as they contain eugenol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease jaw pain.

Buds: Cinnamon buds are used to make essential oils for jaw clenching as they contain compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw.

Stems: Cinnamon stems are used to make essential oils for jaw clenching as they contain compounds like linalool, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease jaw pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its adaptogenic properties that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. This calming effect can help alleviate the physical tension that often contributes to jaw clenching. Additionally, the oil's ability to regulate stress hormones, such as cortisol, can also help to calm the nervous system and prevent muscle tension in the jaw. By reducing overall stress and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata essential oils can provide a natural solution for jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: Isovitexin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often associated with jaw clenching.
Lignans (including pinoresinol and lariciresinol): Lignans, a type of phenolic compound, have a sedative effect, which can help to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension.
Rotundifuran: Rotundifuran, a sesquiterpene, has a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a sedative effect, helping to relax muscles and reduce anxiety-related jaw clenching.

Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata contain passifloric acid and other compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Flowers: The flowers of Passiflora incarnata contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can cause jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the delicate purple petals.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a stainless steel apparatus at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the distillate in a clean glass container to separate the essential oil from water.
Store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid.
Label and record the essential oil's properties and uses for future reference and safe handling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. It contains sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing muscle tension and stress. The oil's aroma also has a grounding effect, helping to calm the mind and promote relaxation. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, Santalum album essential oils can help alleviate jaw clenching and promote overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for tension relief and jaw pain. Its soothing effects can also promote better sleep and reduce anxiety.
remedy_constituents: α-Santalol: Acts as a muscle relaxant, helping to reduce tension in the jaw muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.
β-Santalol: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
Santalal: Shows sedative and anxiolytic effects, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, which can help relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension.
Stems: Utilized for their calming and relaxing properties, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Leaves: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature  santalum album  leaves and twigs for high concentration of essential oil.
Steam distill 400g of harvested  santalum album  material at 100°c for 2 hours.
Separate the distilled essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities and contaminants.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles at 4°c to preserve its quality and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with jaw clenching because its calming and anti-inflammatory properties can reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation in the jaw area. The oil's refreshing scent, often associated with the citrusy aroma of lemongrass, can also help to calm the mind and body, leading to a decrease in stress-related jaw clenching. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus essential oils may help alleviate jaw pain and promote a healthier, more relaxed jaw alignment.
remedy_constituents: Citronellal: It has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help relax tense muscles and reduce jaw clenching.
Limonene: This terpene has anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Geranial: It possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching and temporomandibular joint disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of citral, a compound known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate jaw clenching pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are also used for their citral content, which can help reduce muscle tension and pain associated with jaw clenching.

Stems: The stems are sometimes used to produce essential oil, although to a lesser extent than leaves and rhyzomes, as they may contain lower concentrations of citral, but still have some analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from mature plants in the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at a temperature of 80-100°c for 2-3 hours using a still.
Collect 10-15 ml of essential oil from the distillation process and transfer it to a clean container.
Allow the collected essential oil to settle for 24 hours before decanting it into a dark glass bottle.
Store the herbal essential oil in a cool dark place to preserve its aroma and medicinal properties for 6 months.
